title: Han Solo at Stars' End (comic strip)

"'''''Han Solo at Stars' End'''''" is a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] that was based on the [[Han Solo at Stars' End|novel of the same name]] by [[Brian Daley]]. It was adapted by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Alfredo Alcala]] in [[1980]] and  [[1981]]. It nearly ended up being the last ''Star Wars'' newspaper comic.

The strip has been republished by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[1997]]. It was also on [[StarWars.com]]'s [[Star Wars comic books|webstrips]] section of [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]].

==Plot summary==
Upon discovering that they need a special waiver in order to operate the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] within the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] without running afoul of the authorities, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] attempt to get in touch with [[Klaus Vandangante/Legends|Doc]], an outlaw technician. However, Doc's daughter [[Jessa Vandangante|Jessa]] informs them that Doc has vanished, presumably abducted. Han works out a deal with Jessa: in exchange for the waiver and upgrades to the ''Falcon'', he and Chewbacca will help discover what happened to Doc and other enemies of the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], the capitalistic and authoritarian organization that oversees the Corporate Sector.

As part of this effort, Han and Chewbacca escort two droids, [[BLX-5|Bollux]] and [[Blue Max]], to the planet Orron III, where they meet with [[Rekkon]], the leader of the missing-persons search. Blue Max, a small computer probe hidden in Bollux's chest cavity, infiltrates the Authority's computer network and searches for the location of the abductees. However, Rekkon's small group has been compromised by a traitor, and their activities are discovered.

During the group's attempt to escape the planet, Chewbacca is captured by Espos (Authority [[Security Police/Legends|Security Police]]) and taken away. Han and the others escape the planet aboard the ''Falcon''. During liftoff, the traitor shoots Rekkon and destroys the data that Blue Max gathered. Before dying, however, Rekkon leaves the words "Stars' End, [[Mytus VII/Legends|Mytus VII]]" scrawled on the ''Falcon''{{'s}} game table. Han realizes that this must be the location of the Authority's covert prison facility. After unmasking and dealing with the traitor, Han and the remaining members of Rekkon's group fly to Stars' End. Masquerading as interstellar entertainers, they infiltrate the facility and rescue the kidnap victims, including Doc and Chewbacca. The facility and staff members are destroyed in the escape.

==Development==
This strip almost ended up being the last ''Star Wars'' newspaper strip to run in the ''Los Angeles Times''. In January 1981, the ''LA Times'' dropped the ''Star Wars'' strip, citing lack of interest. The Star Wars Fan Club wrote a letter of protest on the subject, which convinced the ''LA Times'' to allow the strip to continue,<ref name="letter">{{Blogspot|subdomain=dailysw|url=2011/02/star-wars-daily-strips-bonus-content.html|name=Daily Star Wars' 30th Anniversary blog|text=The Star Wars Daily Strips - Bonus Content}}</ref> beginning with ''[[The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell]]''.

The beginning strip for the story arc was later published on the official ''Star Wars'' Tumblr account.<ref name="Tumblr">{{Tumblr|subdomain=starwars|url=post/70607386792/han-solo-at-stars-end-based-on-the-novel-by|name=Star Wars|text=Han Solo at Stars' End}}</ref>
